title: Pearl
summary: The checked-in Pearl XML is a local repository representation of a University of Michigan electronic text whose legacy header names Oxford Text Archive U-1686-A and a 1993 distribution date. The current OTA 1686 record is separately dated 1992-04-03. Their declared immediate source is E. V. Gordon's 1953 Clarendon Press edition, finalized for publication after his death by Ida L. Gordon; that edition represents the unique medieval witness, British Library Cotton MS Nero A X/2. The manuscript, scholarly edition, electronic versions, and repository artifact are modeled separately so that composition, copying, publication, access, foliation, and rights statements remain scoped to the correct layer.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for Pearl
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion, catalog, and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The XML identifies the digital item as Pearl through HEADER/FILEDESC/PUBLICATIONSTMT/IDNO[@TYPE='dlps'].
The source submodule is a distinct git repository; this manifest does not alter the XML.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item Pearl
entities.description: A publicly accessible electronic text of the Middle English poem, structurally divided into twenty sections and associated in the legacy header with Oxford Text Archive U-1686-A.

date_statements.label: electronic distribution date in legacy header
date_statements.notes: The header does not distinguish initial OTA creation, U-M ingest, or later platform migration dates.

notes: The current U-M item exposes the complete poem as web text.
The exact division of encoding responsibility between OTA and U-M is not documented by the item-level evidence reviewed here.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive text 1686, Pearl
entities.description: The current OTA record corresponding to legacy identifier U-1686-A. The official record describes it as an SGML-tagged version of OTA text 581 and provides a header and plain-text file.
notes: The matching U-1686-A identifier ties this record to the local header; no checksum comparison between the OTA files and U-M or local representations was completed.
OTA text 581 is a separately numbered precursor mentioned by this record and should not be collapsed into text 1686.

entities.label: E. V. Gordon edition of Pearl (1953)

bibliographic.citation: Pearl, ed. E. V. Gordon (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1953); final revision after the editor's death by Ida L. Gordon.
bibliographic.title: Pearl

notes: The local XML header abbreviates the extent as 167 pages and excludes the edition's substantial preliminary and apparatus matter from the electronic text.
Internet Archive metadata states that Ida L. Gordon made the final revision after E. V. Gordon's death.
The edition is a Middle English scholarly text, not a modern-language translation.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Nero A X/2
alternate_labels: Cotton MS Nero A X, article 3
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness of Pearl and of Cleanness, Patience, and Sir Gawain and the Green Knight. Pearl occupies modern folios 41r–59v; the older foliation embedded in the repository XML runs 39r–55v.

historical_designations: Cotton MS Nero A X, article 3
Cotton MS Nero A X, ff. 41–130
holding.notes: The original composite Cotton MS Nero A X was separated into two volumes in 1964.

physical_description.extent: 90 folios in the separately bound volume; Pearl at modern folios 41r–59v
physical_description.format: Parchment codex, written in one column of 36 lines
notes: Gothic cursive script described as textura rotunda with anglicana features.
The manuscript has two foliation sequences; the BL catalog uses modern pencil foliation, while the XML milestones preserve the older sequence.
The text was copied by one scribe in a South-East Cheshire or North-East Staffordshire dialect; the poet's dialect is distinguished as farther south in Staffordshire.
Four full-page Pearl drawings at modern folios 41r–42v precede the written poem; the BL catalog describes the dreamer, Pearl Maiden, river, and heavenly Jerusalem scenes.
Do not describe the repository XML's folio milestones as modern BL foliation.
The witness date is separate from the poem's composition date and the later illustration date.

entities.label: University of Calgary Gawain Manuscript microfilm page images
entities.description: A public digital collection of page images made from a microfilm reel of Cotton MS Nero A X/2, covering the manuscript that uniquely contains Pearl.

physical_description.extent: Full digital coverage of Cotton MS Nero A X/2 reported by the British Library catalog
physical_description.format: Digital page images derived from microfilm
notes: Item-level records pair the older and modern foliation sequences and provide attribution and usage fields.
The collection is a reproduction layer, not the manuscript itself and not the repository XML.

agents.name: University of Calgary Libraries and Cultural Resources
Ida L. Gordon
notes: Internet Archive catalog metadata credits her with the final revision after E. V. Gordon's death.

subject.description: The complete checked-in Pearl XML artifact

object.description: The U-M CME digital text identified as Pearl

scope.notes: The local header, item identifier, structure, and text align with U-M's item. The exact download, release, and any intermediate normalization are not documented.

subject.description: The U-M CME Pearl electronic text

scope.notes: The shared U-1686-A identifier establishes a version relationship, but the reviewed records do not prove the direction or mechanics of copying between these electronic representations.

subject.description: The complete electronic poem, excluding omitted editorial matter

object.description: The Middle English text and twenty-section structure in Gordon's edition

scope.notes: The header explicitly names the 1953 Gordon edition and enumerates print components omitted from the electronic text.

subject.description: The edited Middle English poem on text pages 1–44

locators: older foliation 39r–55v in the repository XML

access.access_method: Checked-out CME submodule or pinned GitHub blob
U-M item page and View entire text
The public OTA record lists and previews header1686.xml and pearl-1686.txt, but direct anonymous bitstream requests are disallowed and download-all redirects to OTA login
notes: A successful authenticated download was not verified; registration may not by itself guarantee file access.

access.access_method: Internet Archive controlled digital lending item; sign-in and borrowing availability may be required
notes: The item is in the printdisabled collection and exposes encrypted lending derivatives rather than an unrestricted public PDF.

access.access_method: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue
Request the shelfmark using the BL collection-item request process; consultation is in London and restrictions apply
notes: The catalog states that access restrictions and a letter of introduction apply and directs readers to consult staff.
The current catalog explicitly reports the digital images as unavailable.

access.access_method: University of Calgary Digital Collections Gawain Manuscript collection

rights.component: The Pearl digital text and materials presented by University of Michigan
rights.basis: The item and collection pages expressly state that the materials are in the public domain in the United States and apply No Copyright–United States.
notes: No Copyright–United States is a rights statement, not a worldwide license and not an itemized analysis of Gordon's 1953 editorial contributions.

rights.component: The upstream U-M/OTA transcription and medieval text represented in the checked-in XML
rights.basis: U-M currently marks the corresponding item No Copyright–United States, while the checked-in XML header says the OTA text was made available for personal scholarly use only.
notes: The public-domain assertion and legacy provider restriction are separate facts; this record does not treat the current U-M label as silently superseding the embedded term.
This statement does not independently adjudicate protectable Gordon-edition readings or cover project-authored code, metadata, typography, or outputs.

rights.component: Files distributed through the current OTA text 1686 record
rights.basis: The current official OTA record expressly licenses the item under Creative Commons Attribution 3.0; that license grant is recorded separately from any public-domain status of the medieval text.
notes: This records OTA's current license assertion; it does not independently resolve copyright in Gordon's 1953 editorial contributions or show that the license governs every U-M or repository representation.

rights.component: The anonymous medieval textual content of Pearl
rights.basis: The text is attested in a manuscript copied between 1375 and 1400 and is centuries beyond any U.S. copyright term.
notes: This concerns the old text, not modern photographs, scans, transcriptions, editions, catalog prose, or provider contracts.

rights.component: Protectable readings, introduction, notes, glossary, bibliography, appendixes, typography, and other editorial contributions in Gordon's 1953 edition
rights.basis: A 1953 work required timely renewal to retain an ordinary U.S. term, while certain foreign works may have had protection restored. No item-specific renewal, restoration, exception, or permission was established in this review, and Internet Archive restricts the scan.
notes: The effective-until date is the maximum ordinary 95-year term if protection was secured or restored; it is not a finding that every transcription choice is independently copyrightable.
U-M's NoC-US statement for its digital item is recorded separately and is not silently generalized to the complete printed apparatus.

rights.component: University of Calgary Gawain Manuscript microfilm-derived page images, including Pearl folios
rights.jurisdiction: Provider rights statement and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: A representative University of Calgary item carries a Public Domain label but also enumerates limited usage categories and requires attribution; collection-wide terms and the exact Pearl image records were not verified.
notes: Do not generalize a sampled item's public-domain label to the whole collection or to unrestricted commercial redistribution without reviewing the exact image record and current terms.
Apply the item-level attribution supplied by the provider when using an image.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Preface, table of contents, abbreviations, introduction, bibliography, notes, and appendixes in Gordon's printed edition
editorial_practices.description: The electronic encoding selects the poem and excludes the scholarly edition's prefatory and apparatus material.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete electronic item relative to the 1953 source edition

evidence.summary: The repository describes its working source snapshot as drawn from the Corpus of Middle English.

evidence.citation: General Corpus local Pearl XML header.
evidence.summary: The header identifies Pearl, U-M distributor, item Pearl, OTA U-1686-A, 1993, and E. V. Gordon's 1953 Clarendon Press edition; it lists omitted prefatory and apparatus matter and limits the OTA text to personal scholarly use.

evidence.citation: General Corpus local Pearl XML text.
evidence.locator: TEXT/BODY; twenty DIV1 sections; folio MILESTONE elements
evidence.summary: The complete encoded poem supports its twenty-section content, source-edition page markers 1–44, older manuscript foliation 39r–55v, incipit, and final Amen.

evidence.citation: XPath structural inspection of the General Corpus local Pearl XML.
evidence.locator: Counts of DIV1, LG, L, PB, and MILESTONE elements, including per-section counts
evidence.summary: The XML contains twenty DIV1 sections, 101 LG stanza elements, and 1,212 L elements. Every section has five 12-line stanzas except Section XV, which has six.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, ‘Pearl,’ item Pearl.
evidence.locator: Item information, rights, actions, and contents
evidence.summary: The page identifies Oxford: Clarendon Press 1953, OTA U-1686-A, twenty sections, a complete web text, and No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection home and rights statement.
evidence.locator: Collection description and Rights and Permissions
evidence.summary: U-M describes an initial group of texts obtained from OTA, transcribed by the Library, or contributed by faculty; later batches were manually keyed from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading and basic TEI markup. It offers bulk XML and states No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, text 1686, Pearl, full item record.
evidence.locator: Full item record, files, issue date, abstract, identifier, and rights
evidence.summary: The official record identifies ota:1686 as Pearl, issued 1992-04-03, describes it as an SGML-tagged version of text 581, lists and previews header1686.xml and pearl-1686.txt, and asserts a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 license. Its bitstreams currently disallow anonymous access and download-all redirects to login.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Nero A X/2, record 040-003316720.
evidence.summary: The record supplies the shelfmark, four anonymous poems and modern folios, copying and decoration dates, dialect localizations, physical description, Pearl illustration subjects, current access restrictions, unavailable BL digital images, and a pointer to full Calgary coverage.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item pearlgord00gord, Pearl, ed. E. V. Gordon (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1953).
evidence.summary: The scan metadata confirms the 1953 Clarendon Press edition, E. V. Gordon's editorship, Ida L. Gordon's final revision after his death, bibliography, and access-restricted printdisabled status.

evidence.citation: Review of E. V. Gordon, ed., Pearl, Speculum 29 (1954): 113–115.
evidence.summary: The contemporary scholarly review identifies Gordon's Oxford: Clarendon Press 1953 edition and gives its extent as lx plus 167 pages with a frontispiece.

evidence.citation: University of Calgary Digital Collections, Gawain Manuscript collection and representative Cotton Nero A X/2 item.
evidence.locator: Collection; representative item Details and How can you use this image?
evidence.summary: The public collection supplies Cotton Nero A X/2 microfilm-derived page images. A representative item pairs older and modern foliation, labels Copyright Status Public Domain, lists Usage Rights Education, Instruction, Private study, Research, and supplies attribution wording.
notes: The sampled rights item depicts Gawain rather than Pearl; it documents collection-level presentation practice, while item-specific Pearl attribution should be taken from the exact image record used.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: Works already under statutory protection before 1978; mandatory renewal; restoration of foreign works
evidence.summary: The Copyright Office explains the possible 95-year term for protected pre-1978 publications, mandatory renewal for 1950–1963 works, restoration of certain foreign works, and the current pre-1931 U.S. public-domain cutoff.

open_questions.question: What exact OTA/U-M release, download date, and transformation history produced the pinned local Pearl XML blob?
next_steps: Inspect CME repository history and surviving U-M or OTA release documentation.
Record any intermediate normalization with checksums and dates.

open_questions.question: Was the 1953 Gordon edition's U.S. copyright renewed or restored, which edition components remain protected in intended publication jurisdictions, and how should U-M's NoC-US statement be reconciled with them?
next_steps: Review the edition's copyright page, renewal records, and any URAA restoration evidence.
Compare the XML systematically with public-domain manuscript facsimiles and earlier editions before attributing reusable readings to the 1953 edition.
Obtain legal review before distributing protected apparatus or nontrivial edition-specific readings.

open_questions.question: How do the current U-M and OTA rights statements apply to the distinct U-M, OTA 1686, and checked-in Pearl representations, and do either supersede the personal-scholarly-use-only term embedded in the checked-in XML?
next_steps: Ask U-M and OTA to explain which terms govern each representation and whether the historical restriction remains operative.
Compare OTA 1686, U-M, and checked-in files by checksum and content before asserting a copying direction; keep separately numbered OTA text 581 distinct.
Retain the historical restriction and current provider statements as separate dated layers.

open_questions.question: Do the University of Calgary facsimile's enumerated Usage Rights permit commercial redistribution despite representative item-level Public Domain labels?
next_steps: Review the current Calgary license and terms linked from each exact Pearl image proposed for use.
Request written clarification for commercial print or bundled image redistribution.
Preserve exact item-level attribution and any response.

open_questions.question: Which specific portions of the posthumous 1953 edition were completed or revised by Ida L. Gordon rather than E. V. Gordon?
next_steps: Inspect the edition's preface and archival publication history.
Refine creator roles only when the division of labor is explicitly documented.

review_notes: Composition, manuscript copying, illustration, print publication, electronic distribution, and repository dates are modeled separately.
Older XML foliation 39r–55v and modern BL foliation 41r–59v are both retained with explicit labels.
Access and rights records apply only to the named entity or component; public access to a transcription does not automatically authorize reuse of Gordon's apparatus or manuscript images.
This record deliberately treats the exact OTA-to-U-M-to-repository transfer history and the 1953 edition's rights as unresolved.
